title: The Outcasts. (Melbourne.)
content:
Here to the parks they come,
        The scourings of the town,
     Like weary wounded animals
        Seeking where to lie them down.

        Brothers, let us take together
        An easeful period.
     There is worse than to be as we are - 
        Cast out, not of men but of God!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Francis William Lauderdale Adams, titled "The Outcasts. (Melbourne.)", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
